questions.question: Emma scores these marks in five tests: 8, 9, 7, 9, 9. What is the mode?
questions.explanation: The mode is the value that appears most often. Count each: 7 appears once, 8 appears once, 9 appears three times. The mode is 9. ✓

questions.question: Tom records the temperatures: 12°C, 15°C, 18°C, 14°C, 16°C. What is the mean temperature?
questions.explanation: Mean = sum of all values ÷ number of values. Sum = 12 + 15 + 18 + 14 + 16 = 75. Mean = 75 ÷ 5 = 15°C. ✓

questions.question: Sophie arranges these numbers in order: 3, 5, 7, 9, 11. What is the median?
questions.explanation: The median is the middle value when numbers are in order. The middle number of 3, 5, 7, 9, 11 is 7. ✓

questions.question: Jake measures heights: 140cm, 155cm, 148cm, 162cm, 145cm. What is the range?
questions.explanation: Range = highest value - lowest value. Range = 162 - 140 = 22cm. ✓

questions.question: This bar chart shows Lucy's test scores. What was her score in Science?
questions.explanation: Reading from the bar chart, the Science bar reaches up to 8. ✓

questions.question: Ben records rainfall in mm: 12, 8, 15, 8, 12, 20, 8. What is the mode?
questions.explanation: The mode is the most common value. 8mm appears three times, more than any other value. ✓

questions.question: Mia's test scores are: 6, 8, 10, 12. What is the mean score?
This pie chart shows Oliver's time spent on activities. What fraction of time is spent on Homework?
questions.explanation: The Homework section takes up 90° of the circle. 90° out of 360° = 90/360 = 1/4. ✓

questions.question: Hannah records: 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14. What is the median?
questions.explanation: With an even number of values, the median is the mean of the two middle numbers. The middle values are 8 and 10. Median = (8 + 10) ÷ 2 = 9. ✓

questions.question: This line graph shows temperature during a day. At what time was the temperature highest?
questions.explanation: Reading the line graph, the highest point is at 3pm. ✓

questions.question: Charlie scores: 15, 18, 12, 20, 15. What is the range?
questions.explanation: Range = highest - lowest = 20 - 12 = 8. ✓

questions.question: This table shows bus arrival times. What time does the 10:30am bus arrive at Park Street?
questions.explanation: Reading the timetable, the bus departing at 10:30 from Main Station arrives at Park Street at 10:45. ✓

questions.question: Sarah records ages: 8, 10, 9, 11, 12, 10. What is the mode?
questions.explanation: The mode is the most common value. 10 appears twice, more than any other value. ✓

questions.question: The mean of four numbers is 12. Three of the numbers are 10, 11, and 15. What is the fourth number?
questions.explanation: If the mean is 12 and there are 4 numbers, the total sum must be 12 × 4 = 48. The three known numbers add up to 10 + 11 + 15 = 36. So the fourth number = 48 - 36 = 12. ✓

questions.question: This bar chart shows ice cream sales. How many more vanilla cones were sold than chocolate?
questions.explanation: Reading the bar chart: Vanilla = 45, Chocolate = 35. Difference = 45 - 35 = 10. ✓

questions.question: Lily records temperatures: 18°C, 22°C, 20°C, 24°C, 21°C. What is the median temperature?
questions.explanation: First arrange in order: 18, 20, 21, 22, 24. The middle value is 21°C. ✓

questions.question: This two-way table shows pets owned. How many children own a dog?
questions.explanation: Reading from the table, 18 children own a dog. ✓

questions.question: Max records goals scored: 2, 5, 3, 5, 1, 5, 4. What is the mode and range?
questions.explanation: Mode is 5 (appears three times). Range = highest - lowest = 5 - 1 = 4. ✓

questions.question: This pie chart shows favorite sports. If 120 children were surveyed, how many chose Football?
questions.explanation: Football takes up 120° of the circle. 120° out of 360° = 1/3. So 1/3 of 120 = 40 children. ✓

questions.question: Ella has scores: 7, 8, 9, ?, 11. If the mean is 9, what is the missing score?
questions.explanation: If the mean is 9 and there are 5 numbers, the total sum must be 9 × 5 = 45. The four known numbers add up to 7 + 8 + 9 + 11 = 35. So the missing score = 45 - 35 = 10. ✓

questions.question: This line graph shows plant growth over 6 weeks. How much did the plant grow between week 2 and week 5?
questions.explanation: Reading the graph: Week 2 = 6cm, Week 5 = 18cm. Growth = 18 - 6 = 12cm. ✓

questions.question: Noah records: 3, 6, 9, 12, 15. What is the mean?
This bar chart shows book sales. What was the total number of books sold across all four weeks?
questions.explanation: Reading the bars: Week 1 = 50, Week 2 = 60, Week 3 = 55, Week 4 = 55. Total = 50 + 60 + 55 + 55 = 220. ✓

questions.question: Ava collects data: 5, 8, 5, 12, 5, 9, 15. What is the mode and median?
questions.explanation: Mode = 5 (appears three times). For median, arrange in order: 5, 5, 5, 8, 9, 12, 15. Middle value = 8. ✓

questions.question: This two-way table shows transport methods. How many students walk to school?
questions.explanation: Reading from the table, 40 students walk to school. ✓

questions.question: What is the mean of these numbers? 10, 14, 16, 12, 18
What is the mode? 5, 7, 5, 9, 5, 8
questions.explanation: The mode is the most common value. 5 appears three times, more than any other. ✓

questions.question: What is the median? 4, 8, 12, 16, 20
questions.explanation: The median is the middle value. The middle number is 12. ✓

questions.question: What is the range? 25, 38, 42, 31, 29
questions.explanation: Range = highest - lowest = 42 - 25 = 17. ✓

questions.question: What is the mean? 15, 20, 25, 30
What is the median? 6, 9, 12, 15, 18, 21
questions.explanation: With 6 numbers, median is the mean of middle two: (12 + 15) ÷ 2 = 13.5. ✓

questions.question: What is the range? 18, 32, 45, 28, 19
What is the mode? 8, 10, 8, 12, 10, 8
questions.explanation: 8 appears three times, more than any other. Mode = 8. ✓

What is the median? 11, 14, 17, 20, 23
questions.explanation: The middle value is 17. ✓

questions.question: This bar chart shows weekly sales. What were the sales in Week 3?
questions.explanation: Reading the bar chart, Week 3 sales = 45. ✓

questions.question: What is the mean? 22, 26, 30, 34, 38
What is the mode? 13, 15, 13, 17, 13, 19
questions.explanation: 13 appears three times, more than any other. ✓

questions.question: What is the range? 52, 68, 45, 79, 61

questions.explanation: Reading the graph at 2pm, temperature = 22°C. ✓

questions.question: What is the median? 8, 14, 20, 26, 32, 38
What is the mean? 18, 24, 30, 36
What is the mode? 6, 9, 6, 12, 6, 15
questions.explanation: 6 appears three times. Mode = 6. ✓

questions.question: This pie chart shows children's favourite fruits. What fraction of the children chose Apples?
questions.explanation: Apples section = 120° out of 360° = 120/360 = 1/3. ✓

questions.question: What is the range? 35, 48, 62, 41, 54
What is the median? 16, 22, 28, 34, 40

questions.question: The mean of five numbers is 20. Four are 18, 19, 22, 23. What is the fifth?
This table shows bus times. When does the 8:15am bus arrive at Station B?
questions.explanation: Reading the timetable, 8:15am bus arrives at Station B at 8:40am. ✓

questions.question: What is the mean? 32, 40, 48, 56
What is the mode? 11, 14, 11, 17, 11, 20
questions.explanation: 11 appears three times. Mode = 11. ✓

questions.question: What is the range? 24, 37, 51, 29, 44
This bar chart shows scores. What is the total of all scores?
questions.explanation: Adding all bars: 45 + 50 + 55 + 50 = 200. ✓

questions.question: What is the median? 10, 18, 26, 34, 42, 50
What is the mean? 45, 50, 55, 60, 65
What is the mode and range? 7, 9, 7, 11, 7, 13
questions.explanation: Mode = 7 (appears 3 times). Range = 13 - 7 = 6. ✓

questions.question: This line graph shows growth. How much growth between Day 3 and Day 7?
questions.explanation: Day 3 = 8cm, Day 7 = 18cm. Growth = 18 - 8 = 10cm. ✓

questions.question: What is the median? 19, 25, 31, 37, 43
questions.explanation: The middle value is 31. ✓

questions.question: What is the range? 66, 82, 58, 91, 74
This pie chart shows time spent. If total is 24 hours, how many hours for Sleep?
questions.explanation: Sleep = 120° out of 360° = 1/3. So 24 ÷ 3 = 8 hours. ✓

questions.question: What is the mean? 28, 35, 42, 49, 56
What is the mode? 15, 18, 15, 21, 15, 24
questions.explanation: 15 appears three times. Mode = 15. ✓

questions.question: What is the median? 12, 20, 28, 36, 44, 52
This two-way table shows sports. How many play Tennis?
questions.explanation: Reading from the table, 30 play Tennis. ✓

questions.question: What is the range? 39, 56, 47, 68, 52
The mean of three numbers is 24. Two are 22 and 25. What is the third?
What is the mean? 36, 45, 54, 63
This bar chart shows attendance. How many more attended Friday than Monday?
questions.explanation: Friday = 58, Monday = 46. Difference = 58 - 46 = 12. ✓

questions.question: What is the mode? 19, 22, 19, 25, 19, 28
questions.explanation: 19 appears three times. Mode = 19. ✓

questions.question: What is the median? 21, 28, 35, 42, 49
questions.explanation: The middle value is 35. ✓

questions.question: What is the range? 43, 59, 38, 72, 55
This line graph shows sales. What were sales in Month 5?
questions.explanation: Reading the graph at Month 5, sales = 75. ✓

questions.question: What is the mean? 52, 60, 68, 76, 84
What is the median? 14, 24, 34, 44, 54, 64
What is the mode? 23, 26, 23, 29, 23, 32
questions.explanation: 23 appears three times. Mode = 23. ✓

What is the mean? 44, 52, 60, 68, 76
What is the median? 23, 30, 37, 44, 51
questions.explanation: The middle value is 37. ✓

This two-way table shows preferences. How many chose Pizza?
questions.explanation: Reading from the table, 44 chose Pizza. ✓

questions.question: What is the mode? 27, 30, 27, 33, 27, 36
questions.explanation: 27 appears three times. Mode = 27. ✓

questions.question: What is the range? 48, 67, 42, 79, 56
This bar chart shows production. What was the total production?
questions.explanation: Adding all bars: 65 + 70 + 75 + 70 = 280. ✓

questions.question: What is the mean? 64, 72, 80, 88, 96
What is the median? 16, 26, 36, 46, 56, 66
What is the mode and median? 8, 11, 8, 14, 8, 17
questions.explanation: Mode = 8 (appears 3 times). Order: 8, 8, 8, 11, 14, 17. Median = (8 + 11) ÷ 2 = 9.5. ✓

questions.question: This line graph shows temperature. What is the range of temperatures shown?
questions.explanation: Highest = 26°C, Lowest = 12°C. Range = 26 - 12 = 14°C. ✓

questions.question: What is the range? 52, 71, 45, 84, 59
What is the mean? 55, 65, 75, 85, 95
What is the median? 25, 33, 41, 49, 57
questions.explanation: The middle value is 41. ✓

This pie chart shows colours. What percentage chose Blue?
What is the mode? 31, 34, 31, 37, 31, 40
questions.explanation: 31 appears three times. Mode = 31. ✓

questions.question: What is the range? 61, 78, 54, 92, 69
This table shows scores. What is the median score?
questions.explanation: Reading scores from table and ordering, median = 66. ✓

questions.question: What is the mean? 48, 60, 72, 84, 96
What is the median? 18, 28, 38, 48, 58, 68
What is the mode? 35, 38, 35, 41, 35, 44
questions.explanation: 35 appears three times. Mode = 35. ✓

questions.question: This bar chart shows sales. Which day had the highest sales?
questions.explanation: Reading the bars, Thursday has the tallest bar = highest sales. ✓

questions.question: What is the range? 56, 75, 49, 88, 63
What is the mean? 70, 80, 90, 100, 110
What is the median? 27, 36, 45, 54, 63
questions.explanation: The middle value is 45. ✓

questions.question: The mean of five numbers is 42. Four are 40, 41, 43, 45. What is the fifth?
This line graph shows progress. How much progress between Week 2 and Week 6?
questions.explanation: Week 2 = 25, Week 6 = 43. Progress = 43 - 25 = 18. ✓

questions.question: What is the mode? 39, 42, 39, 45, 39, 48
questions.explanation: The mode is the value that appears most often. In this list, 39 appears three times — more than any other number — so the mode is 39. ✓

questions.question: What is the range? 64, 83, 57, 96, 71
This two-way table shows responses. How many answered 'Yes'?
questions.explanation: Reading from the table, 56 answered 'Yes'. ✓

questions.question: What is the mean? 82, 90, 98, 106, 114
What is the median? 20, 32, 44, 56, 68, 80
What is the mode and range? 12, 15, 12, 18, 12, 21
questions.explanation: Mode = 12 (appears 3 times). Range = 21 - 12 = 9. ✓

questions.question: This pie chart shows subjects. If 180 students surveyed, how many chose Maths?
What is the range? 68, 87, 61, 100, 75
What is the mean? 66, 78, 90, 102, 114
What is the median? 29, 39, 49, 59, 69
questions.explanation: The middle value is 49. ✓

questions.question: This bar chart shows attendance. What is the mean attendance?
What is the mode? 43, 46, 43, 49, 43, 52
questions.explanation: 43 appears three times. Mode = 43. ✓

questions.question: The mean of three numbers is 50. Two are 48 and 51. What is the third?
What is the range? 72, 91, 65, 104, 79
This line graph shows visitors. On which day were there 85 visitors?
questions.explanation: Reading the graph, Wednesday shows 85 visitors. ✓

questions.question: What is the mean? 74, 86, 98, 110, 122
What is the median? 22, 34, 46, 58, 70, 82
What is the mode? 47, 50, 47, 53, 47, 56
questions.explanation: 47 appears three times. Mode = 47. ✓

questions.question: This two-way table shows preferences. What is the total number surveyed?
questions.explanation: Adding all values in the table: total = 170. ✓

questions.question: What is the range? 76, 95, 69, 108, 83
What is the mean? 58, 72, 86, 100, 114
Eight pupils scored the following marks in a maths test: 54, 61, 67, 72, 75, 80, 83, 88. What is the median score?
questions.explanation: With 8 values, the median is the mean of the 4th and 5th values. The data is already in order. The 4th value is 72 and the 5th value is 75. Median = (72 + 75) ÷ 2 = 147 ÷ 2 = 73.5. ✓

questions.question: A school tuck shop recorded the number of each snack sold in one week: Apples 35, Bananas 50, Oranges 20, Grapes 45, Pears 30. How many more Bananas were sold than Oranges?
questions.explanation: Bananas sold = 50. Oranges sold = 20. Difference = 50 − 20 = 30. ✓

questions.question: Find the mode and range of this data set: 3, 8, 5, 3, 9, 3, 7, 12.
questions.explanation: Mode = 3 (it appears 3 times, more than any other value). Range = highest − lowest = 12 − 3 = 9. ✓

questions.question: A cafe recorded the number of sandwiches sold each day: Monday 24, Tuesday 31, Wednesday 18, Thursday 27, Friday 35. What was the total number of sandwiches sold that week?
Seven children recorded how many books they read last month: 14, 9, 22, 17, 5, 30, 11. What is the median number of books?
questions.explanation: First, put the numbers in order: 5, 9, 11, 14, 17, 22, 30. There are 7 values, so the median is the 4th value. Median = 14. ✓

questions.question: A shop records ice cream sales each hour. At 11am they had sold 20 ice creams, at 12pm they had sold 40, and at 1pm they had sold 60. If sales increased at a steady rate, how many had they sold by 12:30pm?
questions.explanation: Between 12pm and 1pm, sales went from 40 to 60 — an increase of 20 in one hour. In half an hour, the increase would be 20 ÷ 2 = 10. So at 12:30pm, sales = 40 + 10 = 50. ✓

questions.question: Six children measured their heights: 132 cm, 145 cm, 128 cm, 151 cm, 139 cm, 147 cm. What is the median height?
questions.explanation: First, order the heights: 128, 132, 139, 145, 147, 151. With 6 values, the median is the mean of the 3rd and 4th values. Median = (139 + 145) ÷ 2 = 284 ÷ 2 = 142 cm. ✓

questions.question: Class A scored these marks in a quiz: 15, 18, 22, 25, 20. Class B scored: 21, 14, 23, 17, 30. Which class had the higher mean score, and by how much?
options: Both classes had the same mean

questions.question: In a survey, children chose their favourite colour: Red — 12, Blue — 18, Green — 9, Yellow — 15, Purple — 6. How many more children chose Blue than Green and Purple combined?
questions.explanation: Green and Purple combined = 9 + 6 = 15. Blue = 18. Difference = 18 − 15 = 3. ✓

questions.question: Sam scored 72, 85, 68, 91 and 79 in five science tests. What is his mean score, and how many tests did he score above the mean?
questions.explanation: Mean = (72 + 85 + 68 + 91 + 79) ÷ 5 = 395 ÷ 5 = 79. Scores above 79: 85 and 91 — that is 2 tests. ✓

questions.question: The mean of 6 numbers is 15. Five of the numbers are 12, 14, 16, 18 and 20. What is the sixth number?
questions.explanation: If the mean is 15 and there are 6 numbers, the total sum = 15 × 6 = 90. The sum of the five known numbers = 12 + 14 + 16 + 18 + 20 = 80. The sixth number = 90 − 80 = 10. ✓

questions.question: A pie chart shows favourite sports for 240 children. Football is 90°, Cricket is 60°, Tennis is 120° and Swimming is 90°. How many children chose Tennis?
questions.explanation: Tennis = 120° out of 360°. Fraction = 120 ÷ 360 = 1/3. Number who chose Tennis = 240 × 1/3 = 80. ✓

questions.question: Five numbers are written in order. The smallest number is 4, the mode is 4, the median is 6, and the range is 9. What is the largest number?
questions.explanation: The range is 9 and the smallest number is 4, so the largest number = 4 + 9 = 13. We can check: since the mode is 4, the number 4 must appear more than once, giving us 4, 4, ?, ?, 13. The median (3rd value) is 6, so the set is 4, 4, 6, ?, 13. This all works. The largest number is 13. ✓

questions.question: A pie chart shows how 300 pupils travel to school. The Bus section has an angle of 108°. The Car section is half the size of the Bus section. How many pupils travel by Car?
questions.explanation: The Bus section is 108°. The Car section is half of that = 108 ÷ 2 = 54°. The fraction for Car = 54 ÷ 360 = 3/20. Number of pupils = 300 × 3/20 = 45. ✓

questions.question: Seven children have heights: 125 cm, 130 cm, 132 cm, 138 cm, 142 cm, 148 cm, 155 cm. If the tallest and shortest children leave the group, what is the mean height of the remaining five children?
questions.explanation: Remove the tallest (155 cm) and shortest (125 cm). Remaining heights: 130, 132, 138, 142, 148. Sum = 130 + 132 + 138 + 142 + 148 = 690. Mean = 690 ÷ 5 = 138 cm. ✓

questions.question: The mean score of 8 pupils in a test is 25. When one pupil's score is removed, the mean of the remaining 7 pupils is 24. What was the removed pupil's score?
questions.explanation: Total for 8 pupils = 25 × 8 = 200. Total for remaining 7 pupils = 24 × 7 = 168. The removed score = 200 − 168 = 32. ✓

questions.question: Four numbers have a mean of 10. When written in order, the first three numbers are 5, 8 and 12. What is the median of these four numbers?
questions.explanation: First, find the fourth number. Mean = 10, so the total = 10 × 4 = 40. Sum of known numbers = 5 + 8 + 12 = 25. Fourth number = 40 − 25 = 15. The four numbers in order: 5, 8, 12, 15. With 4 values, the median is the mean of the 2nd and 3rd values = (8 + 12) ÷ 2 = 10. ✓

questions.question: In a spelling test marked out of 10, the results were: Score 5 — 2 pupils, Score 6 — 4 pupils, Score 7 — 6 pupils, Score 8 — 5 pupils, Score 9 — 3 pupils. What fraction of the class scored 8 or more?
questions.explanation: Total pupils = 2 + 4 + 6 + 5 + 3 = 20. Pupils who scored 8 or more = 5 + 3 = 8. Fraction = 8/20 = 2/5. ✓

questions.question: The mean age of 4 friends is 11 years. A fifth friend joins the group and the new mean age becomes 12 years. How old is the fifth friend?
questions.explanation: Total age of 4 friends = 11 × 4 = 44. Total age of 5 friends = 12 × 5 = 60. Age of fifth friend = 60 − 44 = 16. ✓

questions.question: In a class of 30 children, the mean score on a test was 64 marks. The 18 girls had a mean score of 68 marks. What was the mean score of the boys?
questions.explanation: Total marks for all 30 children = 30 × 64 = 1,920. Total marks for 18 girls = 18 × 68 = 1,224. Number of boys = 30 − 18 = 12. Total marks for boys = 1,920 − 1,224 = 696. Mean score of boys = 696 ÷ 12 = 58. ✓

questions.question: The bar chart shows the favourite sports of children in Year 5. How many children chose football?
questions.explanation: Reading the bar for Football, it reaches 12 on the vertical axis. So 12 children chose football. ✓

questions.question: The bar chart shows the favourite school dinners of children in Class 6B. Which was the most popular school dinner?
questions.explanation: The tallest bar is for Pizza, which has a value of 25. This is higher than all the other bars. So Pizza was the most popular school dinner. ✓

questions.question: The bar chart shows the results of a pet survey in Year 5. How many more children have a dog than a rabbit?
questions.explanation: Dog = 18 children. Rabbit = 6 children. Difference = 18 − 6 = 12. So 12 more children have a dog than a rabbit. ✓

questions.question: The bar chart shows the favourite ice cream flavours chosen by children at a school fair. Which flavour was the least popular?
questions.explanation: The shortest bar is for Toffee, with only 5 children choosing it. This is fewer than all the other flavours. So Toffee was the least popular. ✓

questions.question: The bar chart shows how children in Year 5 travel to school. How many children travel by car or bus?
questions.explanation: Car = 12 children. Bus = 8 children. Total = 12 + 8 = 20 children travel by car or bus. ✓

questions.question: The bar chart shows the number of books borrowed from the school library in one week. What is the total number of books borrowed?
questions.explanation: Total = 24 + 16 + 20 + 8 + 12 = 80 books were borrowed in total. ✓

questions.question: The bar chart shows the favourite sports of 50 children. What fraction of the children chose swimming?
questions.explanation: Swimming = 10 children. Total = 15 + 10 + 5 + 10 + 10 = 50 children. Fraction = 10/50 = 1/5. ✓

questions.question: The bar chart shows ice cream sales at a shop each day. On which day were sales double Tuesday's sales?
questions.explanation: Tuesday's sales = 8 ice creams. Double Tuesday's sales = 8 × 2 = 16. Looking at the chart, Thursday had 16 sales. So Thursday's sales were double Tuesday's. ✓

questions.question: The bar chart shows the favourite break-time snacks chosen by children at Oakwood Primary. How many more children chose Crisps and Fruit combined than Biscuits and Yoghurt combined?
questions.explanation: Crisps + Fruit = 30 + 25 = 55. Biscuits + Yoghurt = 20 + 15 = 35. Difference = 55 − 35 = 20. ✓

questions.question: The bar chart shows the favourite colours chosen by Year 5 children. What fraction of the children chose either Red or Yellow?
questions.explanation: Red = 14, Yellow = 10. Combined = 14 + 10 = 24. Total children = 14 + 22 + 8 + 10 + 6 = 60. Fraction = 24/60 = 2/5. ✓

questions.question: The bar chart shows the number of sandwiches sold at a café each day. What is the mean number of sandwiches sold per day?
questions.explanation: Total = 45 + 30 + 55 + 40 + 50 = 220. Mean = 220 ÷ 5 = 44 sandwiches per day. ✓

questions.question: The bar chart shows the favourite sports of children at a school. If 25% of all the children surveyed chose Football, how many children were surveyed in total?
questions.explanation: Football = 20 children, and this is 25% of the total. If 25% = 20, then 1% = 20 ÷ 25 = 0.8. So 100% = 0.8 × 100 = 80 children were surveyed in total. ✓

questions.question: The bar chart shows the mean test scores for five subjects. What is the overall mean score across all five subjects?
questions.explanation: Total of all mean scores = 72 + 68 + 80 + 64 + 76 = 360. Overall mean = 360 ÷ 5 = 72%. ✓

questions.question: The bar chart shows cakes sold at a bakery each day. The baker wants the mean daily sales to be 26. By how many cakes did the actual mean fall short of the target?
questions.explanation: Total cakes sold = 18 + 24 + 12 + 30 + 36 = 120. Actual mean = 120 ÷ 5 = 24. Target mean = 26. Shortfall = 26 − 24 = 2 cakes. ✓

questions.question: The bar chart shows how 120 children travel to school. What percentage of the children travel by Car?
questions.explanation: Car = 45 children. Total = 120 children. Percentage = (45 ÷ 120) × 100 = 37.5%. ✓

questions.question: The bar chart shows fruit sold at a school tuck shop. Each piece of fruit costs 40p. What was the total income from selling Apples and Pears?
questions.explanation: Apples sold = 35. Pears sold = 30. Total pieces = 35 + 30 = 65. Each costs 40p. Total income = 65 × 40p = 2600p = £26.00. ✓

questions.question: The table shows the favourite sports of children in Year 5 and Year 6. How many Year 5 children chose football?
questions.explanation: Looking at the Year 5 row and the Football column, the value is 12. So 12 Year 5 children chose football. ✓

questions.question: The table shows the favourite school dinners of boys and girls. What is the total number of children who chose Pasta?
questions.explanation: Pasta: Boys = 11, Girls = 13. Total = 11 + 13 = 24 children chose Pasta. ✓

questions.question: The table shows how many library books each class borrowed last week. How many books did Class 5B borrow in total?
questions.explanation: Class 5B: Fiction = 7, Non-fiction = 8, Comics = 5. Total = 7 + 8 + 5 = 20 books. ✓

questions.question: The table shows the favourite pets of children in Year 4 and Year 5. Which pet was most popular with Year 4 children?
questions.explanation: Year 4: Cat = 8, Dog = 12, Rabbit = 3. The largest number is 12 for Dog. So Dog was the most popular pet with Year 4 children. ✓

questions.question: The table shows the favourite sports of boys and girls. How many more girls than boys chose swimming?
questions.explanation: Girls who chose Swimming = 20. Boys who chose Swimming = 14. Difference = 20 − 14 = 6. So 6 more girls than boys chose swimming. ✓

questions.question: The table shows lunch choices for Year 5 and Year 6. What fraction of Year 5 children chose Sandwiches?
questions.explanation: Year 5 total = 15 + 20 + 5 = 40. Year 5 Sandwiches = 15. Fraction = 15/40. Simplify by dividing both by 5: 15 ÷ 5 = 3, 40 ÷ 5 = 8. So the fraction is 3/8. ✓

questions.question: The table shows favourite book types chosen by boys and girls. How many children chose either Mystery or Fantasy in total?
questions.explanation: Mystery: Boys = 8, Girls = 14, total = 22. Fantasy: Boys = 12, Girls = 8, total = 20. Mystery + Fantasy combined = 22 + 20 = 42 children. ✓

questions.question: The table shows how Year 5 and Year 6 children travel to school. What fraction of all children surveyed travel by bus?
The table shows the favourite ice cream flavours of boys and girls. Which flavour had the biggest difference between boys and girls?
questions.explanation: Chocolate: Boys = 20, Girls = 14, difference = 6. Vanilla: Boys = 12, Girls = 16, difference = 4. Strawberry: Boys = 5, Girls = 13, difference = 8. Strawberry had the biggest difference of 8. ✓

questions.question: The table shows favourite sports of children in Year 5 and Year 6. What percentage of all children surveyed chose Football?
The table shows lunch choices of boys and girls. What is the mean number of children per lunch choice?
questions.explanation: Pasta total = 10 + 14 = 24. Curry total = 8 + 6 = 14. Jacket potato total = 12 + 8 = 20. Salad total = 2 + 4 = 6. Mean = (24 + 14 + 20 + 6) ÷ 4 = 64 ÷ 4 = 16 children per lunch choice. ✓

questions.question: The table shows library books borrowed by Year 5 and Year 6 last month. What percentage of Year 6's books were Non-fiction?
questions.explanation: Year 6 Non-fiction = 18. Year 6 total = 12 + 18 + 10 = 40. Percentage = (18 ÷ 40) × 100 = 45%. ✓

questions.question: The table shows how many children in each class chose their favourite subject. What percentage of all children chose English? Round to the nearest whole number.
The table shows favourite snacks for Year 5 and Year 6. Each snack costs 35p. If every child bought their favourite snack, what would be the total spent by Year 6 children?
questions.explanation: Year 6 total children = 14 + 22 + 8 + 6 = 50. Each snack costs 35p. Total = 50 × 35p = 1750p = £17.50. ✓

questions.question: The pie chart shows the favourite sports of children in a class. What fraction of the children chose Football?
questions.explanation: Football takes up exactly half of the pie chart. The fraction is 1/2. ✓

questions.question: The pie chart shows how children travel to school. Which way of travelling to school is the most popular?
questions.explanation: Walk has the largest section of the pie chart (1/3), so it is the most popular way of travelling to school. ✓

questions.question: The pie chart shows the favourite subjects of pupils in Year 5. Which two subjects are equally popular?
questions.explanation: Maths and English each take up 1/4 of the pie chart. They have equal-sized sections, so they are equally popular. ✓

questions.question: The pie chart shows favourite ice cream flavours. Which flavour is the least popular?
questions.explanation: Mint has the smallest section of the pie chart (1/10), so it is the least popular flavour. ✓

questions.question: 120 children were asked about their favourite sport. The pie chart shows the results. How many children chose Tennis?
questions.explanation: Tennis takes up 1/5 of the pie chart. 1/5 of 120 = 120 ÷ 5 = 24 children. ✓

questions.question: 60 pupils were asked how they travel to school. The pie chart shows the results. How many more pupils travel by Car than by Bus?
questions.explanation: Car = 1/3 of 60 = 20 pupils. Bus = 1/6 of 60 = 10 pupils. Difference = 20 − 10 = 10 more pupils travel by Car. ✓

questions.question: The pie chart shows how children in Year 5 spend their free time. What percentage of children chose Sport?
questions.explanation: The Sport section takes up 72° out of 360°. As a percentage: 72 ÷ 360 × 100 = 20%. ✓

questions.question: A family spends £240 in a month. The pie chart shows how the money is split. How much is spent on Food and Clothes combined?
200 people were surveyed about their favourite sport. Football is 1/4 and Tennis is 1/5 of the total. How many people chose neither Football nor Tennis?
questions.explanation: Football = 1/4 of 200 = 50 people. Tennis = 1/5 of 200 = 40 people. Football + Tennis = 50 + 40 = 90 people. Neither = 200 − 90 = 110 people. ✓

questions.question: 240 pupils were asked how they travel to school. The pie chart shows that 1/3 walk and 1/4 come by Car. How many pupils travel by Bus or Cycle altogether?
questions.explanation: Walk = 1/3 of 240 = 80 pupils. Car = 1/4 of 240 = 60 pupils. Walk + Car = 80 + 60 = 140 pupils. Bus or Cycle = 240 − 140 = 100 pupils. ✓

questions.question: 180 pupils chose their favourite subject. Maths is 1/4, English is 1/5, and Science is 1/6 of the total. The rest chose History or Other. If twice as many chose Other as chose History, how many chose History?
questions.explanation: Maths = 1/4 of 180 = 45. English = 1/5 of 180 = 36. Science = 1/6 of 180 = 30. Total for those three = 45 + 36 + 30 = 111. Remaining = 180 − 111 = 69 pupils chose History or Other. If Other is twice History, let History = x. Then x + 2x = 69, so 3x = 69, x = 23 pupils chose History. ✓

questions.question: The line graph shows the temperature during one day. What was the temperature at 2pm?
questions.explanation: Reading the graph at 2pm, the temperature was 22°C. The highlighted point confirms this. ✓

questions.question: The line graph shows the temperature recorded every hour. At what time was the temperature highest?
questions.explanation: Looking at the graph, the highest point is at 2pm when the temperature reached 21°C. ✓

questions.question: The line graph shows how tall a sunflower grew over five weeks. How tall was the sunflower at the end of Week 3?
questions.explanation: Reading the graph at Week 3, the sunflower was 9 cm tall. ✓

questions.question: The line graph shows the temperature during a summer day. How much did the temperature rise between 10am and 2pm?
questions.explanation: At 10am the temperature was 13°C. At 2pm it was 24°C. Rise = 24 − 13 = 11°C. ✓

questions.question: The line graph shows the number of visitors to a library each day. Between which two consecutive days was the biggest increase in visitors?
questions.explanation: Mon to Tue: 38 − 45 = −7 (decrease). Tue to Wed: 52 − 38 = 14. Wed to Thu: 65 − 52 = 13. Thu to Fri: 80 − 65 = 15. The biggest increase was Thursday to Friday with 15 more visitors. ✓

questions.question: The line graph shows the number of ice creams sold by a shop each month. How many more ice creams were sold in June than in March?
questions.explanation: In June, 80 ice creams were sold. In March, 25 were sold. Difference = 80 − 25 = 55 more ice creams. ✓

questions.question: The line graph shows a bean plant's height over 6 weeks. Between which two consecutive weeks did the plant grow the most?
questions.explanation: Week 1 to 2: 5 − 2 = 3 cm. Week 2 to 3: 10 − 5 = 5 cm. Week 3 to 4: 16 − 10 = 6 cm. Week 4 to 5: 20 − 16 = 4 cm. Week 5 to 6: 28 − 20 = 8 cm. The biggest growth was Week 5 to 6 with 8 cm. ✓

questions.question: The line graph shows the temperature from 9am to 1pm. What is the mean temperature across all five readings?
The temperature rises at a steady rate, as shown in the line graph. If the pattern continues, what would you expect the temperature to be at 4pm?
questions.explanation: The temperature rises by 4°C each hour (8, 12, 16, 20, 24). At 3pm it would be 24 + 4 = 28°C. At 4pm it would be 28 + 4 = 32°C. ✓

questions.question: The line graph shows library visitors each day. The librarian says the mean number of visitors per day was at least 115. Is this correct? What is the actual mean?
questions.explanation: Total visitors = 120 + 95 + 110 + 140 + 85 = 550. Mean = 550 ÷ 5 = 110. The librarian said at least 115, but the actual mean is 110, so the librarian is not correct. ✓

questions.question: The line graph shows ice cream sales over 6 months. What fraction of the total ice creams sold were sold in the first three months (January to March)?
questions.explanation: First three months: 20 + 25 + 35 = 80. Total for all six months: 20 + 25 + 35 + 50 + 70 + 100 = 300. Fraction = 80/300. Simplify by dividing both by 20: 4/15. ✓

questions.question: The range of 5 numbers is 22. Four of the numbers are 10, 15, 22, and 25. What is the fifth number?
questions.explanation: The range = highest − lowest = 22. The current highest is 25 and the current lowest is 10, giving a range of only 15. We need a range of 22, so the fifth number must extend the spread. If the fifth number is below 10: 25 − fifth = 22, so fifth = 3. Check: the set is 3, 10, 15, 22, 25 and the range = 25 − 3 = 22 ✓. The fifth number is 3. ✓

questions.question: Set A has numbers: 14, 22, 18, 25, 10. Set B has numbers: 33, 19, 27, 41, 30. What is the difference between the range of Set B and the range of Set A?
questions.explanation: Set A: highest = 25, lowest = 10, range = 25 − 10 = 15. Set B: highest = 41, lowest = 19, range = 41 − 19 = 22. Difference = 22 − 15 = 7. ✓

questions.question: Six children scored these marks in a test: 12, 18, 25, 36, ?, 49. The range is 37. After removing the highest score, the range decreases by 7. What is the missing score?
questions.explanation: Original range = 49 − 12 = 37 ✓. After removing the highest score (49), the new range = 37 − 7 = 30. The lowest is still 12, so the new highest must be 12 + 30 = 42. The missing score is 42. Check: without 49, the data is 12, 18, 25, 36, 42 and the range = 42 − 12 = 30 ✓. ✓

questions.question: Oliver records the temperature at noon each day for a week: 8°C, 11°C, 5°C, 14°C, 9°C, 3°C, 12°C. He removes the day with the lowest temperature. By how much does the range decrease?
questions.explanation: Sorted data: 3, 5, 8, 9, 11, 12, 14. Original range = 14 − 3 = 11. After removing the lowest (3°C), the data is: 5, 8, 9, 11, 12, 14. New range = 14 − 5 = 9. Decrease = 11 − 9 = 2. ✓

questions.question: A set of 7 numbers has a mode of 5. Six of the numbers are 3, 5, 7, 5, 4, and 8. What is the seventh number?
questions.explanation: The mode is the most common number. Currently, 5 appears twice and all other numbers appear once. For 5 to be the only mode, the seventh number must be 5 (making it appear 3 times). If the seventh number were 3, 4, 7, or 8, that number would also appear twice — tying with 5 — so 5 would not be the single mode. The seventh number is 5. ✓

questions.question: Which of these data sets has exactly two modes (is bimodal)?
questions.explanation: A bimodal set has exactly two values appearing most often. Option A: 3 appears twice, all others once — one mode. Option B: 2 and 3 each appear twice, all others once — two modes, bimodal ✓. Option C: all values appear once — no mode. Option D: 6 appears three times — one mode. Option E: 1, 2, and 3 each appear twice — three modes, not two. The answer is 1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 4. ✓

questions.question: A set of 5 numbers has a mode of 6 and a mean of 5. Three of the numbers are 3, 4, and 6. What are the other two numbers?
questions.explanation: The mode is 6, so 6 must appear more than any other number. It already appears once, so at least one missing number must be 6. The mean is 5 with 5 numbers, so the total = 5 × 5 = 25. Sum of known numbers = 3 + 4 + 6 = 13. The two missing numbers sum to 25 − 13 = 12. If one is 6, the other = 12 − 6 = 6. So both are 6. Check: 3, 4, 6, 6, 6 — mode = 6 (appears 3 times) ✓, mean = 25 ÷ 5 = 5 ✓. The answer is 6 and 6. ✓

questions.question: Amelia has 9 number cards. The mode is 7 and the median is 7. The smallest number is 2 and the range is 10. She has exactly three 7s. What is the largest number?
questions.explanation: The smallest number is 2 and the range is 10, so the largest number = 2 + 10 = 12. Check: with 9 cards, the median is the 5th value. Having three 7s ensures 7 is at the middle position and is the mode (appearing more than any other value). The largest number is 12. ✓

questions.question: Eleven pupils scored the following marks: 45, 52, 38, 61, 73, 55, 49, 67, 42, 58, 70. What is the median score?
questions.explanation: First put the scores in order: 38, 42, 45, 49, 52, 55, 58, 61, 67, 70, 73. With 11 values, the median is the 6th value. Counting: 38(1st), 42(2nd), 45(3rd), 49(4th), 52(5th), 55(6th). The median is 55. ✓

questions.question: Five numbers are in order: 2, 7, ?, 13, 18. The median is the same as the mean. What is the middle number?
questions.explanation: With 5 numbers, the median is the 3rd value, which is the missing number. Call it m. The mean = (2 + 7 + m + 13 + 18) ÷ 5 = (40 + m) ÷ 5. Since median = mean: m = (40 + m) ÷ 5. Multiply both sides by 5: 5m = 40 + m. Subtract m: 4m = 40. So m = 10. Check: the set is 2, 7, 10, 13, 18. Mean = 50 ÷ 5 = 10. Median = 10 ✓. ✓

questions.question: Seven numbers have a median of 15. The numbers in order are: 6, 9, 12, 15, 19, 23, 28. If a new number is added and the median becomes 17, which number was added?
questions.explanation: With 7 numbers, the median is the 4th value = 15 ✓. Adding one number gives 8 values, so the new median is the mean of the 4th and 5th values, which must equal 17. So the 4th + 5th values must sum to 34. If we add 19: the sorted list is 6, 9, 12, 15, 19, 19, 23, 28. The 4th value is 15 and the 5th value is 19. Median = (15 + 19) ÷ 2 = 34 ÷ 2 = 17 ✓. The number added was 19. ✓

questions.question: Nine numbers have a median of 12. The numbers in order are: 3, 5, 8, 10, 12, 14, 17, 20, 25. If the smallest number is removed, what is the new median?
questions.explanation: Removing 3 leaves 8 values: 5, 8, 10, 12, 14, 17, 20, 25. With 8 values, the median is the mean of the 4th and 5th values. The 4th value is 12 and the 5th value is 14. Median = (12 + 14) ÷ 2 = 26 ÷ 2 = 13. ✓

questions.question: Find the median of these four numbers: 3, 5, 7, 9.
questions.explanation: With 4 numbers (an even count), the median is the mean of the 2nd and 3rd values. The numbers in order: 3, 5, 7, 9. The 2nd value is 5 and the 3rd value is 7. Median = (5 + 7) ÷ 2 = 12 ÷ 2 = 6. ✓

questions.question: Find the median of these six numbers: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12.
questions.explanation: With 6 numbers (an even count), the median is the mean of the 3rd and 4th values. The numbers in order: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12. The 3rd value is 6 and the 4th value is 8. Median = (6 + 8) ÷ 2 = 14 ÷ 2 = 7. ✓

questions.question: Eight numbers have a median of 15.5. Seven of the numbers are: 8, 11, 13, 15, 18, 21, 24. What is the eighth number?
questions.explanation: With 8 numbers, the median is the mean of the 4th and 5th values, which must equal 15.5. So the 4th + 5th values must sum to 31. The seven known numbers in order: 8, 11, 13, 15, 18, 21, 24. If the eighth number is 16, the sorted list is: 8, 11, 13, 15, 16, 18, 21, 24. The 4th value is 15 and the 5th value is 16. Median = (15 + 16) ÷ 2 = 31 ÷ 2 = 15.5 ✓. The eighth number is 16. ✓

questions.question: Ten numbers are in order. The median is 20.5. The 5th number is 18 and the 7th number is 25. What is the 6th number?
questions.explanation: With 10 numbers, the median is the mean of the 5th and 6th values. Median = (5th + 6th) ÷ 2 = 20.5. So 5th + 6th = 41. The 5th number is 18, so the 6th number = 41 − 18 = 23. Check: 23 is between the 5th (18) and 7th (25) values ✓. ✓

questions.question: Three numbers have a mean of 6. Two of the numbers are 5 and 7. What is the third number?
questions.explanation: If the mean of 3 numbers is 6, the total = 6 × 3 = 18. The two known numbers sum to 5 + 7 = 12. The third number = 18 − 12 = 6. ✓

questions.question: Three numbers have a mean of 10. Two of the numbers are 8 and 12. What is the third number?
questions.explanation: If the mean of 3 numbers is 10, the total = 10 × 3 = 30. The two known numbers sum to 8 + 12 = 20. The third number = 30 − 20 = 10. ✓

questions.question: Four numbers have a mean of 10. Three of the numbers are 8, 12, and 9. What is the fourth number?
questions.explanation: If the mean of 4 numbers is 10, the total = 10 × 4 = 40. The three known numbers sum to 8 + 12 + 9 = 29. The fourth number = 40 − 29 = 11. ✓

questions.question: Five numbers have a mean of 15. Four of the numbers are 12, 18, 14, and 20. What is the fifth number?
questions.explanation: If the mean of 5 numbers is 15, the total = 15 × 5 = 75. The four known numbers sum to 12 + 18 + 14 + 20 = 64. The fifth number = 75 − 64 = 11. ✓

questions.question: Find both the mode and the range of this data set: 3, 5, 3, 7, 3, 9.
questions.explanation: Mode = 3 (it appears 3 times, more than any other number). Range = highest − lowest = 9 − 3 = 6. So the mode is 3 and the range is 6. ✓

questions.question: Find both the mean and the mode of this data set: 4, 6, 4, 8, 4, 10.
questions.explanation: Mode = 4 (it appears 3 times, more than any other number). Mean = (4 + 6 + 4 + 8 + 4 + 10) ÷ 6 = 36 ÷ 6 = 6. So the mean is 6 and the mode is 4. ✓

questions.question: A set of 5 numbers has a mode of 4, a median of 5, and a range of 8. The numbers are all whole numbers. What is the largest number?
questions.explanation: The mode is 4, so 4 appears more than once. With 5 numbers, the median is the 3rd value = 5. Since 4 < 5, both 4s must be in the first two positions: 4, 4, 5, ?, ?. The range is 8. The smallest number is 4, so the largest = 4 + 8 = 12. Check: 4, 4, 5, ?, 12 — we need the 4th number to be between 5 and 12 and not equal to 4. For example, 4, 4, 5, 7, 12 works: mode = 4 ✓, median = 5 ✓, range = 12 − 4 = 8 ✓. The largest number is 12. ✓
